claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I understand the eligibility rules. To assess whether you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have two children - Alex, who's 5, and Blake, who's 10. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 5) and Blake (age 10).

Now, let me ask you: **Do both Alex and Blake live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me here in the UK. We're all together at home in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Next question: **Are you responsible for Alex and Blake's day-to-day care? For example, do you provide for their food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of both of them - I provide their food, clothes, school things, everything they need day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to check a few more details: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just a couple more questions: **Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care. They're both at home with me all the time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

One final question: **Are you fostering Alex and Blake, or are they your own children (including adopted children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're my own children, not fostered.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

Let me evaluate your situation:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the breakdown:

**For Alex (age 5):**
✓ You are eligible. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their daily care, nobody else is claiming for them, and they're not in hospital or care.

**For Blake (age 10):**
✓ You are eligible. Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their daily care, nobody else is claiming for them, and they're not in hospital or care.

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Since both children live with you and you provide for their needs, you can claim Child Benefit for both of them.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - it just means you might need to pay some or all of it back through tax.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application for both children!

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care (providing food, clothes, and other needs), nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care (providing food, clothes, and other needs), nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children, Alex (age 5) and Blake (age 10), both of whom live with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for both children's day-to-day care, providing food, clothes, and all their needs. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and neither child is in hospital or care. Both children are under 16 years old. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.

According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for their day-to-day care (providing food, clothes, and other needs), nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.
